text.title: Deploy Notes Template with Rollback Core Rules
text.summary: 7 testable rules grounding the Deploy Notes Template with Rollback methodology. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the last rule binds the decision tree skip path.

rule.statement: Notes MUST pin the exact deployable id (git SHA, image digest, package version). Branch names or 'latest' tags are rejected.
rule.rationale: branch names move; SHAs do not.

rule.statement: Rollback recipe MUST be one of: revert-SHA + redeploy, image-tag rollback, lockfile-pin downgrade, DB-migration down-script. 'Restore from backup' is rejected unless backup is <1h old AND tested in last 30d.
Notes name the impacted surfaces (routes, queues, jobs, tables). Empty blast-radius is rejected; if truly zero, mark `dry_run=true`.
rule.rationale: incident response without surface map is guessing.

rule.statement: Notes list ≥2 post-deploy verification steps with explicit pass criteria (curl a health endpoint, check a metric, run a smoke). 'Looks good' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Successful deploys without verification become next week's incidents.

rule.statement: Notes declare the rollback window (how long before rollback becomes impossible). Open-ended windows are rejected.

rationale.source: Backwards-incompatible changes (DB drop, external webhook config) close windows fast

rule.statement: Notes carry a single named operator. Generic owners are rejected.

When the decision tree routes to `skip-this-methodology`, the agent MUST emit a structured `skip` decision (with reason + suggested-sibling) rather than running Deploy Notes Template with Rollback under degraded conditions.
rule.rationale: decision-tree contract. The decision tree is the only place that knows the routing; skipping must be observable so the orchestrator can log the routed-elsewhere path.
